title: Oenothera eastwoodiae
intro: Oenothera eastwoodiae, commonly known as Eastwood's evening primrose, is a perennial flowering plant species belonging to the family Onagraceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and skin conditions such as eczema. The bioactive constituents of Oenothera eastwoodiae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Oenothera eastwoodiae is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is used to support relaxation and promote a restful sleep.
taxonomy: Oenothera eastwoodiae is classified within the family Onagraceae, commonly known as the evening primrose family. It belongs to the genus Oenothera and is a species of flowering plant. Specifically, Oenothera eastwoodiae is classified as a perennial plant that is native to the western United States. The plant is further categorized as a member of the tribe Onagreae, within the subfamily Onagroideae. Further classification details may be found in various botanical sources and databases.
